title_en: China’s trade gap is narrowing. And other surprises
title_zh: 中国的贸易逆差正在收窄。还有其他意外
rubric_en: The world’s second-biggest economy is stumbling into fiscal austerity
rubric_zh: 世界第二大经济体正在步入财政紧缩境地

paragraphs.en: China’s GDP growth figures are often suspiciously smooth. Last year the government set a target of 5%—and the sprawling $19trn economy duly grew by just that amount, as if the combined endeavours of 760m workers and 30m corporations could be choreographed by a committee. This year the government has permitted itself half a percentage point of drama, setting a target range of 4.5-5%. Few think the official outcome will depart far from this interval.
paragraphs.zh: 中国的GDP增长数字常常显得诡异地平稳。去年政府定下5%的目标，而这座拥有约1.9万亿美元规模（约19万亿美元）且规模庞大的经济体正好增长了这个百分点，仿佛760万? 不，760m（7.6亿）名劳动者和3000万家企业的合力都能由一个委员会统一编排。今年政府给自己留了半个百分点的“戏剧性”空间，设定了4.5%到5%的目标区间。很少有人认为官方结果会离这个区间太远。

paragraphs.en: The GDP numbers for the second quarter, released on July 15th, therefore raised a few eyebrows. The economy grew by 4.3%, year on year. That is hardly a disaster. But it was slower than expected and the weakest since 2022, when China was still imposing citywide lockdowns to fight covid-19.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，7月15日公布的第二季度GDP数据让不少人皱起了眉头。按同比口径，经济增长了4.3%。这绝不是一场灾难，但它低于预期，也是自2022年以来最慢的一次增长——当时中国仍在全国范围内实施封城式封控以抗击新冠病毒。

paragraphs.en: Even that disappointing number would not have been possible without help from abroad. Last year’s trade surplus exceeded $1.2trn (see chart 1). And in June goods exports surged by more than a quarter in dollar terms, year on year. As containerfuls of merchandise chug towards their destinations, China’s trading partners, especially the European Union, are bracing themselves for a second “China shock” akin to the upheaval after the country’s entry into the World Trade Organisation in 2001.
paragraphs.zh: 即便是这个让人失望的数字，也不可能在没有外部助力的情况下出现。去年的贸易顺差超过了1.2万亿美元（见图表1）。而在6月，货物出口按美元计算同比增长超过四分之一。随着满满一船货柜驶向目的地，中国的贸易伙伴，尤其是欧盟，正在为“第二次中国冲击”做准备，这次类似于该国于2001年加入世界贸易组织后引发的巨大震荡。

paragraphs.en: But a closer look reveals something different. “China’s trade surplus has peaked,” concludes Adam Wolfe of Absolute Strategy Research, a firm of analysts. In June imports rose faster than exports, by 36%. The surplus in the first half of 2026 was lower, in dollar terms, than a year before.
paragraphs.zh: 但再仔细看就会看到不一样的情况。Absolute Strategy Research 的分析师Adam Wolfe指出，“中国的贸易顺差已经见顶”。6月进口增长快于出口，涨幅为36%。按美元计量，2026年上半年顺差低于一年前。

paragraphs.en: The Iran war is only partly responsible for the turning-point. China is, of course, paying more for oil than it did last year. But it has softened the blow by dramatically cutting the volume of its imports. In explaining the diminishing surplus, crude matters less than chips. China is both a big exporter and a big importer of semiconductors. Its imports of integrated circuits in May, for example, were up by some 70% year on year in dollar terms. That rise was entirely due to higher chip prices.
paragraphs.zh: 伊朗战争仅是转折点形成的部分原因。中国当然比去年为石油支付了更多费用，但它通过大幅削减进口数量来缓冲了影响。解释顺差收窄时，石油作用反而比不上芯片。中国既是半导体的大出口国，也是大进口国。以集成电路为例，中国5月的进口额同比增长了约70%，这全部是由芯片价格上涨所致。

paragraphs.en: China’s overdependence on exports has been clear for some time. Many analysts therefore hope its leaders will turn again to fiscal stimulus to lift domestic spending. Meanwhile, the opposite is happening. The government is stumbling into “de facto austerity”, as Xiangrong Yu and Xinyu Ji of Citigroup, a bank, have put it.
paragraphs.zh: 中国过度依赖出口的问题已经很久以来都很明显。因此，许多分析人士因此希望其领导层再次动用财政刺激措施来提振国内消费。与此同时，发生的却是相反的局面。正如花旗银行的于翔荣和季欣宇所说，政府正“不由自主地”走向事实上的财政紧缩。

paragraphs.en: Tax revenues have grown strongly in recent months. From January to May the authorities gathered 6.2% more in value-added tax and 12.2% more in personal-income taxes than they did a year before. Thanks to energetic trading in China’s stock markets, stamp duties also brought in far more than usual, rising by 89%.
paragraphs.zh: 税收在最近几个月明显增长。1月至5月，政府共征收的增值税比去年同期多6.2%，个人所得税多12.2%。得益于中国股市的活跃交易，印花税的收入也高于往常，增长了89%。

paragraphs.en: Some of this reflects the return of inflation, which increases the nominal value of purchases, boosting VAT. The tax authorities have also put more effort into enforcement. Since last year, for instance, automated text messages have instructed taxpayers to declare all their overseas income and assets going back to 2022.
paragraphs.zh: 其中一部分反映了通胀回升，购买金额在名义上提高，从而推高了增值税。税务部门也加大了征管力度。比如自去年起，税务部门会通过自动短信要求纳税人申报其所有境外收入和资产，并追溯到2022年。

paragraphs.en: So although the central government is spending more, it is also collecting more. The budget deficit, combining central and local governments, has narrowed a little over the past 12 months. That is the opposite of what stimulus requires—and of what a weak economy needs.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，尽管中央政府的支出在增加，但它也在收更多税。过去12个月里，中央和地方政府合并后的财政赤字有所收窄。这与刺激政策所需要的方向相反，也与疲弱经济所需要的方向相反。

paragraphs.en: The mix of fiscal spending has also changed in counterintuitive ways. China’s leader, Xi Jinping, champions high-tech manufacturing, calling on entrepreneurs and local officials to cultivate “new productive forces”. He has also warned in the past against “welfarism”, arguing that handouts can make people lazy. You might assume, then, that public spending had drifted towards technology and education and away from social safety-nets.
paragraphs.zh: 财政支出结构也以一种不直观的方式发生了变化。中国国家主席习近平大力推动高科技制造业，呼吁企业家和地方官员培育“新的生产力”。他过去也曾警告“福利主义”，认为发放补贴会让人变得懒散。由此你可能会猜测，公共支出已经从社会保障网转向科技和教育。

paragraphs.en: But as Citi’s economists point out, the emerging fiscal “pecking order” seems instead to favour social security (which includes pensions, unemployment insurance, anti-poverty handouts and efforts to put people back to work). These items’ share of the government’s main budget has grown in recent years (see chart 2). The slice devoted to technology and education has remained steady, and the share ploughed into infrastructure has declined.
paragraphs.zh: 但正如花旗的经济学家指出，正在出现的财政“优先顺序”似乎反而偏向社会保障（包括养老金、失业保险、反贫困救济以及帮助失业者重新就业的措施）。近年来，这些项目在政府主要预算中的占比有所上升（见图表2）。而用于科技和教育的预算份额则基本持平，基础设施投入的占比则在下降。

paragraphs.en: This picture does not capture everything. It leaves out China’s state-owned enterprises. It also excludes special government funds, which have their own missions and money. Nonetheless, the numbers reveal some fiscal home truths. In a weak economy and an ageing society, the humdrum demands of the elderly, the unemployed and the poor will always make their presence felt, whatever the leader’s preferences. Chinese technology may be white-hot. But its economy is cooling. And its population is increasingly grey.■
paragraphs.zh: 这个图景并未反映全部情况，它忽略了中国的国有企业，也排除了设有专门任务和资金的专项政府基金。尽管如此，数据也揭示了一些财政上的现实：在经济疲软和老龄化社会中，无论领导层偏好如何，老年人、失业者和贫困人口这些看似普通却不容忽视的需求，始终会持续存在。中国的科技或许正处于白热化阶段，但经济正在降温。并且其人口结构也正在进一步老龄化。■

paragraphs.zh: 如果你想获取更多关于经济、金融和市场领域最重要新闻的专家分析，请订阅《Money Talks》——我们每周的付费订阅通讯。
